Name: Causal Autoimmune Architecture Platform
Description: Rulebook for inferring the complete causal architecture of heterogeneous autoimmune disease from multi-omic cohort data, producing falsifiable mechanisms and ancestry-equitable predictions with calibrated uncertainty.

schema.name: CaseNarrative
schema.Description: PANEL 1 of the witness (raw leaf, read by nothing downstream): the natural-language intake case the LLM was handed. Every raw observation below is extracted from THIS text; each leaf's SourceQuote points back into it so the extraction is human-checkable independently of the verdict. Synthetic but transparent — invented case, literature-aligned loci/thresholds.

schema.Description: Number of serology panels for this individual.

schema.name: CountPreNephriticSignaturePanels
schema.Description: How many of this individual's panels exhibit the pre-nephritic serology signature (rising anti-dsDNA + falling complement before overt nephritis). The corpus-level roll-up of the per-panel signal; emergent from the raw series.

schema.name: IsInPreNephriticSignatureCluster
schema.Description: DERIVED cluster membership: TRUE when this individual's own raw serology series ever showed the pre-nephritic signature. The discovery 'this serology signature precedes nephritis' becomes a reproducible, witnessed corpus-level cluster — not a label anyone assigned.

schema.name: SignatureStrength
schema.Description: DERIVED 0/1/2 strength of the pre-nephritic signature: 2 when it appears on >=2 panels (persistent), 1 on exactly one panel, 0 when absent. Drives the emphasis of cluster members on the cohort scatter.

schema.name: MaxProgressionStateOrder
schema.Description: Highest progression-state order reached across this individual's serology panels (worst/current state).

schema.name: LatestSledaiScore
schema.Description: Peak SLEDAI activity score across this individual's panels (derived).

schema.name: NephritisProgressionStateKey
schema.Description: DERIVED current disease state for the lupus-nephritis-progression machine: decoded from the highest state order reached. The subject- state column of the state machine; never hand-set.

schema.name: ActivityTier
schema.Description: Derived disease-activity tier from peak SLEDAI score.

schema.name: IsHighDiseaseActivity
schema.Description: TRUE when peak SLEDAI >= 12.

schema.name: IsDiseaseProgressing
schema.Description: TRUE when the disease-state simulator places the patient in an active/worsening renal state (independent of the actionability gate).

schema.name: TargetPathwayCode
schema.Description: Resolved pathway code from this individual's causal mechanism (MAXIFS).

schema.name: TargetPathway
schema.Description: Decoded druggable pathway implicated by this individual's mechanism.

schema.name: CurrentProgressionStateId
schema.Description: This individual's current lupus-progression state as a MachineStates id (closure-view key form): the lupus-nephritis-progression machine prefix + the lowercased NephritisProgressionStateKey. Pure projection of the derived current state; used to look up closure-derived prognostics.

schema.name: ReachableStatesAhead
schema.Description: How many disease states are still reachable ahead of THIS patient from their current state, via the transition closure (looks up MachineStates.ReachableStateCount for CurrentProgressionStateId). A closure-derived prognostic horizon: Diego at BiopsyIndicated has 0 ahead (terminal); a Presymptomatic patient has 5. NOT derivable from the severity rank alone (the count is non-monotonic in rank because the machine branches into remission).

data.Name: Ana Reyes
data.CaseNarrative: Ana Reyes, a 34-year-old woman of European ancestry (cohort is in the training set), presents with photosensitive malar rash, arthralgia, and a positive ANA, raising concern for SLE onset. Family history is notable for lupus in a maternal aunt. Genotyping finds the IRF5 regulatory variant rs2004640 at an allele frequency of 0.006 (rare), and allele-specific expression is present at the locus. Functional workup of the IRF5 -> type-I interferon -> SLE-risk edge returns a cis-eQTL in blood RNA-seq with effect 0.91 (SE 0.20), a caQTL in blood ATAC-seq with effect 0.73 (SE 0.19), and a pQTL in blood proteomics with effect 0.66 (SE 0.20) — three assays across two omics modalities, all ancestry-PC and batch adjusted. The edge replicates with a positive effect sign in an East Asian cohort (p=0.004) and in an African cohort (p=0.013), while a European replication is directionally consistent but not significant (p=0.18). Two ancestry-permutation negative controls collapse to the null (permutation effect 0.012 and 0.028, well inside the +/-0.1 null band). The IL-interferon target is druggable (anifrolumab, anti-IFNAR1), giving a perturbable intervention target. For SLE-onset risk, the model's reliability bins each carry coverage of 29-34 held-out individuals, with observed event rates tracking predicted bands closely (e.g. 0.09 observed at the 0.1 band, 0.88 at the 0.9 band). No cryptic-relatedness or assortative-mating leakage was flagged.

data.Name: Bili Okafor
data.CaseNarrative: Bili Okafor, a 41-year-old man of European ancestry (in the training set), presents with serositis, fatigue, and a high-titre ANA suggestive of SLE onset. Genotyping finds the IRF5 regulatory variant rs2004640 at an allele frequency of 0.006 (rare), with allele-specific expression present. The IRF5 -> type-I interferon -> SLE-risk edge is supported by a cis-eQTL in blood RNA-seq with effect 0.84 (SE 0.21), a caQTL in blood ATAC-seq with effect 0.79 (SE 0.20), and a pQTL in blood proteomics with effect 0.58 (SE 0.20), spanning two omics modalities and adjusted for ancestry PCs and batch. It replicates positive in an East Asian cohort (p=0.006) and an African cohort (p=0.017), with a non-significant European replication (p=0.22). Two ancestry-permutation controls collapse to the null (permutation effect 0.015 and 0.024 inside +/-0.1). The interferon target is druggable. The mechanism is as solid as Ana's — but the calibration substrate is thin: this site only accrued coverage of 7-14 held-out individuals per reliability bin, far below the 20-count floor needed to trust the predicted-vs-observed match. No cryptic-relatedness leakage was flagged.

data.Name: Chen Wei
data.CaseNarrative: Chen Wei, a 47-year-old woman of East Asian ancestry (in the training set), presents with arthritis, oral ulcers, and anti-dsDNA positivity concerning for SLE onset. Genotyping finds the STAT4 regulatory variant rs7574865 at an allele frequency of 0.008 (rare), with allele-specific expression present. The STAT4 -> Th1/IFN -> SLE-risk edge shows a cis-eQTL in blood RNA-seq with effect 1.02 (SE 0.23), a caQTL in blood ATAC-seq with effect 0.61 (SE 0.20), and a pQTL in blood proteomics with effect 0.55 (SE 0.21), across two modalities, ancestry-PC and batch adjusted. Replication is troubling: the effect is positive in an East Asian cohort (p=0.009) but the SIGN FLIPS to negative in an African cohort (p=0.024), and the European replication is non-significant (p=0.27) — only one concordant cohort. Worse, while one ancestry-permutation control collapses (permutation effect 0.013), a second negative control does NOT collapse: its permutation effect is 0.46, far outside the +/-0.1 null band, so the signal is not separable from permuted noise. Reliability bins carry coverage of 28-32 per bin. No cryptic-relatedness leakage was flagged.

data.Name: Diego Santos
data.CaseNarrative: Diego Santos, a 29-year-old man of European ancestry (in the training set), presents with nephritis and a malar rash concerning for SLE onset. Genotyping finds the IRF5 regulatory variant rs2004640 at an allele frequency of 0.006 (rare), with allele-specific expression present. The IRF5 -> type-I interferon -> SLE-risk edge is fully supported: a cis-eQTL in blood RNA-seq with effect 0.88 (SE 0.20), a caQTL in blood ATAC-seq with effect 0.95 (SE 0.21), and a pQTL in blood proteomics with effect 0.62 (SE 0.20), across two modalities and adjusted for ancestry PCs and batch. It replicates positive in an East Asian cohort (p=0.003) and an African cohort (p=0.019), with a non-significant European replication (p=0.16). Two ancestry-permutation controls collapse to the null (permutation effect 0.009 and 0.031 inside +/-0.1). The interferon target is druggable, and reliability bins carry coverage of 30-36 per bin. However, sample QC raised a cryptic-relatedness / assortative-mating flag for this participant: kinship estimates indicate undeclared relatives inside the cohort, so the prediction may be confounded by leakage rather than the mechanism alone.

data.Name: Esi Mensah
data.CaseNarrative: Esi Mensah, a 38-year-old woman of African ancestry (in the training set), presents with arthralgia and cytopenias concerning for SLE onset. Genotyping finds the CTLA4 enhancer variant rs3087243 at an allele frequency of 0.007 (rare), with allele-specific expression present. The CTLA4 -> T-cell costimulation -> SLE-risk edge is well supported: a cis-eQTL in blood RNA-seq with effect 0.79 (SE 0.21), a caQTL in blood ATAC-seq with effect 0.71 (SE 0.20), and a pQTL in blood proteomics with effect 0.41 (SE 0.20), across two modalities, ancestry-PC and batch adjusted. It replicates positive in an East Asian cohort (p=0.007) and a European cohort (p=0.021), with a non-significant African replication (p=0.19). Two ancestry-permutation controls collapse to the null (permutation effect 0.011 and 0.026 inside +/-0.1), and reliability bins carry coverage of 27-33 per bin. The gap is experimental: no perturbable intervention target has been mapped for this edge — there is no agent or assay that could knock the costimulation node down to test it, so the mechanism is not experimentally falsifiable as stated.

data.Name: Faisal Haidar
data.CaseNarrative: Faisal Haidar, a 31-year-old man of Indigenous American ancestry — an ancestry deliberately HELD OUT of the training set — presents with dactylitis, enthesitis, and nail pitting concerning for psoriatic-arthritis (PsA) onset. Genotyping finds the IL23R regulatory variant rs11209026 at an allele frequency of 0.009 (rare), with allele-specific expression present. The IL23R -> IL-17 axis -> PsA-risk edge is well supported: a cis-eQTL in blood RNA-seq with effect 1.07 (SE 0.20), a caQTL in blood ATAC-seq with effect 0.83 (SE 0.21), and a pQTL in blood proteomics with effect 0.74 (SE 0.20), across two modalities and adjusted for ancestry PCs and batch. The IL-17 target is druggable (secukinumab). Two ancestry-permutation controls collapse to the null (permutation effect 0.008 and 0.022 inside +/-0.1), and reliability bins carry coverage of 30-35 per bin. But every replication of this edge was run in an Indigenous American cohort: p=0.005, p=0.015, and p=0.034, all positive, all in the SAME ancestry. There is no cohort in a DIFFERENT ancestry confirming the effect — so for a patient whose ancestry is absent from training, nothing demonstrates the mechanism transports. No cryptic-relatedness leakage was flagged.

data.Name: Grace Lin
data.CaseNarrative: Grace Lin, a 36-year-old woman of Indigenous American ancestry — again an ancestry HELD OUT of the training set — presents with plaque psoriasis and asymmetric oligoarthritis concerning for psoriatic-arthritis (PsA) onset. Genotyping finds the IL23R regulatory variant rs11209026 at an allele frequency of 0.009 (rare), with allele-specific expression present. The IL23R -> IL-17 axis -> PsA-risk edge is well supported: a cis-eQTL in blood RNA-seq with effect 0.96 (SE 0.20), a caQTL in blood ATAC-seq with effect 0.88 (SE 0.21), and a pQTL in blood proteomics with effect 0.69 (SE 0.20), across two modalities and adjusted for ancestry PCs and batch. The IL-17 target is druggable (secukinumab). Two ancestry-permutation controls collapse to the null (permutation effect 0.007 and 0.019 inside +/-0.1), and reliability bins carry coverage of 30-36 per bin. Crucially — and unlike Faisal — this edge was MEASURED to replicate ACROSS ancestries: positive in a European cohort (p=0.004), positive in an East Asian cohort (p=0.012), and positive in an Indigenous American cohort (p=0.029). Because the mechanism demonstrably holds in ancestries other than the held-out one, the prediction transports. No cryptic-relatedness leakage was flagged.

data.Name: Hana Yamamoto
data.CaseNarrative: 33yo East Asian woman; rising anti-dsDNA with falling complement and new significant proteinuria (0.7 g/day) over one quarter — early lupus nephritis. Progression-demo case (no v1 keystone mechanism attached).

data.Name: Ibrahim Conteh
data.CaseNarrative: 45yo African man; brisk serologic activity and proteinuria climbing to 1.5 g/day — at risk of renal flare. Progression-demo case (no v1 keystone mechanism attached).

data.Name: Jamal Brooks
data.CaseNarrative: 52yo Hispanic/Latino man; the targeted mechanism is confirmed but the calibration bins are under-covered — uncertainty collapses, so NOT actionable on the calibration gate.

data.Name: Kavya Nair
data.CaseNarrative: 39yo South Asian woman, ancestry absent from training; confirmed mechanism but all replications ran in one ancestry — fails ancestry transport, NOT actionable.

data.Name: Lena Brandt
data.CaseNarrative: 28yo Indigenous-American woman, holdout ancestry; the positive twin — the IL23R node replicated cross-ancestry, so transport passes and she is actionable.

schema.name: IdentificationAssumption

CausalMechanisms.Description: Inferred causal mechanisms linking variants, exposures, and molecular state to clinical phenotypes; must be experimentally falsifiable.

schema.name: CausalMechanismId
schema.Description: Stable identifier for each causal mechanism hypothesis.

schema.name: MechanismLabel
schema.Description: Human-readable mechanism description.

schema.name: Individual
schema.Description: Individual for whom mechanism is inferred.

schema.name: GenomicVariant
schema.Description: Primary genomic variant if applicable.

schema.name: EnvironmentalExposure
schema.Description: Environmental exposure if gene-environment interaction.

schema.name: MechanismType
schema.Description: Type: regulatory, coding, gene-environment-microbiome, epigenetic-inheritance, enhancer-promoter, feedback.

schema.name: TargetPathway
schema.Description: NEW RAW LEAF: druggable pathway this confirmed mechanism implicates (type-I-IFN / B-cell/autoantibody / T-cell-costim / IL-17/23). Drives treatment-line selection. An observation about the mechanism, not derived.

schema.name: TargetPathwayCode
schema.Description: Numeric encoding of TargetPathway so the individual can resolve it via a MAXIFS aggregation (transpiler-friendly). 1=type-I-IFN 2=B-cell 3=T-cell 4=IL-17/23.

schema.name: HasPleiotropy
schema.Description: True when mechanism shows pleiotropy across phenotypes.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: ParentPath
schema.Description: Lookup: Individuals.RelativePath via Individual — used to chain this entity's path under its parent.

schema.name: RelativePath
schema.Description: Path to this CausalMechanism page, chained under its Individual parent.

schema.name: IndividualAncestryLabel
schema.Description: Ancestry lookup.

schema.name: CountQualifiedEvidence
schema.Description: Count of qualified evidence items supporting this mechanism.

schema.name: CountModalitiesSupporting
schema.Description: Count of qualified cross-modality evidence items (multi-omic corroboration).

schema.name: CountInterventionTargets
schema.Description: Count of perturbable intervention targets for this mechanism (falsifiability requires >=1).

schema.name: IsExperimentallyFalsifiable
schema.Description: Derived: a measurable qualified effect exists AND a named intervention can perturb it.

schema.name: CountReplications
schema.Description: Total cross-cohort re-tests of this mechanism.

schema.name: CountConcordantReplications
schema.Description: Re-tests reproducing the predicted sign at significance.

schema.name: CountCrossAncestryConcordant
schema.Description: Concordant re-tests that ran in a DIFFERENT ancestry (the transportability measurement).

schema.name: ReplicationFraction
schema.Description: Derived: fraction of re-tests that were concordant (guarded division).

schema.name: ReplicatesAcrossCohorts
schema.Description: Derived: >=2 independent re-tests and >=2 concordant.

schema.name: CountNegControlTests
schema.Description: Negative-control tests run on this mechanism.

schema.name: CountNegControlSurvived
schema.Description: Negative-control tests the mechanism survived (collapsed under the null).

schema.name: SurvivesNegativeControls
schema.Description: Derived: at least one control run AND all of them survived.

schema.name: IsSpuriousDerived
schema.Description: Derived: spurious unless replicated, survives controls, has >=2 modalities, and is not purely pleiotropic.

schema.name: CausalConfidence
schema.Description: Derived bounded blend of qualified-evidence count, modality breadth, replication rate, and control survival.
schema.formula: =IF(((0.30 * IF({{CountQualifiedEvidence}} >= 4, 1, {{CountQualifiedEvidence}} / 4)) + (0.20 * IF({{CountModalitiesSupporting}} >= 3, 1, {{CountModalitiesSupporting}} / 3)) + (0.30 * {{ReplicationFraction}}) + (0.20 * IF({{SurvivesNegativeControls}}, 1, 0))) > 1, 1, (0.30 * IF({{CountQualifiedEvidence}} >= 4, 1, {{CountQualifiedEvidence}} / 4)) + (0.20 * IF({{CountModalitiesSupporting}} >= 3, 1, {{CountModalitiesSupporting}} / 3)) + (0.30 * {{ReplicationFraction}}) + (0.20 * IF({{SurvivesNegativeControls}}, 1, 0)))

schema.name: VariantIsCausalCandidate
schema.Description: Whether the linked variant is itself a derived causal candidate (empty-guarded).

schema.name: IsCausalArchitectureNode
schema.Description: Derived: a confirmed causal edge - confident, falsifiable, non-spurious, and grounded in a candidate variant or a real exposure.

schema.name: IsAncestryTransportable

schema.Description: Derived: inherits falsifiability - every confirmed node required IsExperimentallyFalsifiable.

schema.name: IsTransportableToAbsentAncestry
schema.Description: Derived: a holdout individual is transportable only with >=1 cross-ancestry-replicated node and no spurious flag.

schema.name: IsAncestryTransportSafe
schema.Description: Derived: holdout requires measured transport; in-training is vacuously safe.

schema.name: TransportGateStatus
schema.Description: RENDER ONLY (does NOT feed the keystone): honest three-state view of the transport gate so a vacuous in-training pass is never shown as evidentiary. NotApplicable = in-training ancestry (gate did not bite); PASS-tested = holdout with a confirmed cross-ancestry transport; FAIL = holdout without one. Sits beside IsAncestryTransportSafe (which the keystone still reads) purely to keep the writeup from implying transport evidence it never used.

schema.name: IsHighConfidencePrediction
schema.Description: Derived: calibrated AND not spurious.

schema.name: PatientStratificationTier
schema.Description: Derived risk tier from the derived PredictedValue.

schema.name: PredictedSeverityValue
schema.Description: Derived severity prediction grounded in the individual's max clinical SeverityScore.

schema.name: SeverityTier
schema.Description: Derived severity band from the predicted severity value.

schema.name: IsSeverityActionable
schema.Description: Derived: a high-severity phenotype on a confirmed, non-spurious mechanism. Chained to the onset gates so severity can never be actionable on a debunked mechanism.

schema.name: SeverityDecidingFactor
schema.Description: Why severity is/ isn't actionable — the single deciding reason.

schema.name: IsTreatmentResponseActionable
schema.Description: Derived: the individual has a treatment predicted to respond (effective therapy on a confirmed mechanism). The third prediction type — independent of onset/severity.

schema.name: TreatmentResponseDecidingFactor
schema.Description: Why treatment-response is/ isn't actionable for this individual.

schema.name: IsClinicallyActionable
schema.Description: KEYSTONE: TRUE only when the prediction is high-confidence (calibrated + not spurious), falsifiability-backed, ancestry-transport-safe, and rests on a non-null derived magnitude.

schema.name: LifecycleStateKey
schema.Description: DERIVED current lifecycle state (never entered): the single deciding gate determines whether the case lands on Actionable or NotActionable. Subject-state column of the diagnosis-lifecycle machine.

schema.name: DecidingGate
schema.Description: DERIVED single primary deciding gate (never entered), named in keystone-AND priority order. 'AllGatesPass' when actionable. When the case rests on no validated mechanism (no confirmed causal node), Falsifiability, Confidence, and Magnitude are one and the same finding, reported as 'NoValidatedMechanism' rather than split across three gates. Otherwise the lone failing gate is named: CrypticRelatedness, Calibration, AncestryTransport.

Axioms.Description: Non-negotiable invariants the platform must obey. Load-bearing constraints, not per-loop work. Captured from the gauntlet conversation.

schema.name: AxiomId
schema.Description: Stable identifier for the axiom.

schema.name: Statement
schema.Description: The invariant, stated as a rule.

schema.name: Rationale
schema.Description: Why this must hold / what it defends against.

schema.name: Category
schema.Description: Grouping: trust-boundary | solve-by-inference | knob | witness | scope | report-derivation.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: RelativePath
schema.Description: Path to this Axioms entry: /admin/axioms/<id>.

data.Statement: The LLM never gets a vote on the conclusion.
data.Rationale: Demotes the model to intake-clerk + lab; the verdict is the deterministic DAG's, never the model's.
data.Name: The LLM never gets a vote on the conclusion.

data.Statement: IsClinicallyActionable is computed, never entered.
data.Rationale: No field a curator (human or LLM) can set to make the diagnosis come out right.
data.Name: IsClinicallyActionable is computed, never entered.

data.Statement: The trust boundary is a line in the DAG: fields with a formula are the model's; raw-input leaf fields are the LLM's (or a human's).
data.Rationale: Makes the boundary mechanically checkable rather than a vibe.
data.Name: The trust boundary is a line in the DAG: fields with a formula are the model's; raw-input leaf fields are the LLM's (or a human's).

data.Statement: The LLM does exactly two things, both at the leaf layer: interpret intake facts, and produce synthetic-but-transparent test results.
data.Rationale: Intake clerk + lab — the un-alarming medical roles. It computes no higher-order inference.
data.Name: The LLM does exactly two things, both at the leaf layer: interpret intake facts, and produce synthetic-but-transparent test results.

data.Statement: Every choice the LLM makes is a literal, editable knob in the admin UI.
data.Rationale: The model's judgment is fully externalized; a wrong input is nudged, not re-prompted.
data.Name: Every choice the LLM makes is a literal, editable knob in the admin UI.

data.Statement: Every DAG formula is inspectable and correctable in place, like a spreadsheet cell.
data.Rationale: Editing a value or formula body is local to its dependency subtree; only retyping/regraphing ripples.
data.Name: Every DAG formula is inspectable and correctable in place, like a spreadsheet cell.

data.Statement: Extraction and derivation are verifiable independently (three-panel witness).
data.Rationale: Input case text -> extracted facts w/ provenance -> deterministic diagnosis. Defeats the laundering objection.
data.Name: Extraction and derivation are verifiable independently (three-panel witness).

data.Statement: The same deterministic machine must land on either side and name the single deciding gate.
data.Rationale: An engine that only ever says "actionable" proves nothing.
data.Name: The same deterministic machine must land on either side and name the single deciding gate.

data.Statement: The diagnosis report is derived, never authored as prose; it is structured natural language assembled from the DAG.
data.Rationale: A prose writeup would be a second, unwitnessed surface where a hallucination could hide. The report must be a deterministic projection of facts already in the graph, the same way Postgres views are.
data.Name: The diagnosis report is derived, never authored as prose; it is structured natural language assembled from the DAG.

data.Statement: Every word the patient gave us, and every node linked to that patient in the DAG down to ground truth, is represented somewhere in that patient's report.
data.Rationale: Completeness is the whole point: nothing the patient said and no derived fact may silently vanish from the writeup. If a value mattered enough to enter the DAG, it earns a sentence (or a deliberate, predicate-suppressed absence).
data.Name: Every word the patient gave us, and every node linked to that patient in the DAG down to ground truth, is represented somewhere in that patient's report.

data.Statement: No single piece of report language is more complex than one spreadsheet cell: it references sibling values directly and does no joins or group-bys.
data.Rationale: CMCC/SDLAF discipline. A narrative fragment is a calculated field on a row; it reads other fields of that same row (and global phrases from __meta), never traverses or aggregates. Complex sentences are decomposed into constituent fragment fields, each one cell-simple.
data.Name: No single piece of report language is more complex than one spreadsheet cell: it references sibling values directly and does no joins or group-bys.

data.Statement: A fragment's language only appears when its predicates hold for that row; irrelevant language is suppressed per case.
data.Rationale: Case-by-case, not a globally imposed template. The same field yields "normal temperature", "light fever", or "severe fever" depending on the row's own value; a fragment whose predicate is false renders empty and contributes nothing to that patient's report.
data.Name: A fragment's language only appears when its predicates hold for that row; irrelevant language is suppressed per case.

data.Statement: Complex language is built from intermediate translation fields: calculated text fragments composed from other predicates on the same row, then composed upward.
data.Rationale: The way to keep every piece cell-simple is to pull a complex sentence apart into its constituent fragments, each its own field, and let higher fragments reference lower ones. The report is the top of that composition, not a monolith.
data.Name: Complex language is built from intermediate translation fields: calculated text fragments composed from other predicates on the same row, then composed upward.

data.Statement: Common, reusable language lives once in the __meta settings table and is referenced by fragments; it is not re-implemented per case.
data.Rationale: Literal global phrases ("The patient presented with…") and shared banding vocabulary belong in __meta so they are authored and corrected in one place. Fragments reference them; the per-row logic stays contextual while the wording stays DRY.
data.Name: Common, reusable language lives once in the __meta settings table and is referenced by fragments; it is not re-implemented per case.

data.Statement: The schema may be expressed in RuleSpeak-style language, but the diagnosis report itself is derived (rendered), not declared.
data.Rationale: RuleSpeak documents the rules; it is not the patient-facing artifact. The report is a rendered projection of each patient's row values through the fragment fields — generated by a thin renderer that only concatenates already-derived, predicate-gated fragments in order.
data.Name: The schema may be expressed in RuleSpeak-style language, but the diagnosis report itself is derived (rendered), not declared.

data.Claim: Raw facts alone derive the keystone with no hand-entered answer anywhere.
data.HowWitnessed: Every leaf in the harness is a raw observation; keystone == AND(gates) cross-check.
data.Name: Raw facts alone derive the keystone with no hand-entered answer anywhere.

data.Claim: Extracted leaf facts are checkable against the source case text, independently of the verdict.
data.HowWitnessed: Three-panel witness view: per-fact provenance pointer back into the case text.
data.Name: Extracted leaf facts are checkable against the source case text, independently of the verdict.

data.Claim: All seven oracle patients resolve to their expected keystone value.
data.HowWitnessed: Build verification asserts the scenarios.md oracle against vw_individualpredictions.
data.Name: All seven oracle patients resolve to their expected keystone value.

data.Claim: Each derived node carries an expert-verifiable line of reasoning.
data.HowWitnessed: Derivation trace from derivation-spec.md printed in every test name/failure.
data.Name: Each derived node carries an expert-verifiable line of reasoning.

data.Claim: Every word the patient gave us appears somewhere in that patient's derived report (no patient input is dropped).
data.HowWitnessed: Coverage check: tokenize each patient's source intake text; assert each meaningful token is traceable into at least one rendered report fragment (directly quoted or its derived translation).
data.Name: Every word the patient gave us appears somewhere in that patient's derived report (no patient input is dropped).

data.Claim: Every DAG node linked to a patient, down to ground truth, is represented somewhere in that patient's report.
data.HowWitnessed: Walk the patient's subgraph (keystone -> gates -> evidence -> raw leaves); assert each visited node contributes at least one rendered fragment, or is deliberately suppressed by a false predicate that the report names.
data.Name: Every DAG node linked to a patient, down to ground truth, is represented somewhere in that patient's report.

data.Claim: No narrative fragment field traverses, joins, or aggregates; each reads only sibling fields of its own row plus __meta__ phrases.
data.HowWitnessed: Static lint over fragment formulas: reject any formula that references another table or a rollup/lookup across rows; fragments may reference {{SiblingField}} and __meta__ keys only.
data.Name: No narrative fragment field traverses, joins, or aggregates; each reads only sibling fields of its own row plus __meta__ phrases.

data.Claim: The same patient row always renders the byte-identical report; the report is a pure projection of the DAG with no model in the loop.
data.HowWitnessed: Render each patient twice and diff; assert zero difference and that the renderer makes no LLM/network calls.
data.Name: The same patient row always renders the byte-identical report; the report is a pure projection of the DAG with no model in the loop.

Features.Description: Catalog of buildable capabilities the platform has / allows for. Coarser grain than loops. Each row carries challenge provenance (ChallengeRefs: exact quoted text + file/line/col into THE-ORIGINAL-CHALLENGE.md, for UI hover tips), a Category, a Priority, and a free-form Markdown ChallengeNotes comment. Source of truth for the DERIVED PLATFORM_FEATURES.md. AssignedLoop links a feature to the loop that delivers it (nullable until scheduled).

schema.name: FeatureId
schema.Description: Stable identifier.

schema.name: Title
schema.Description: Short feature name.

schema.name: Category
schema.Description: Which layer of the platform this belongs to (Keystone / inference DAG, Leaf / LLM surface, Witness / harness, Disease-state simulation, Treatment reasoning, Corpus-level discovery, Reporting, Platform infrastructure).

schema.name: Priority
schema.Description: Load-bearing | Supporting | Roadmap.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: What the capability does (factual, not a pitch).

schema.name: ChallengeRefs
schema.Description: JSON array of provenance pointers into THE-ORIGINAL-CHALLENGE.md. Each: {file,line,col,len,quote,section,relation}. col is the 0-based char offset within the line; len is the quote length; section is prompt|payoff|audit|response; relation is direct|indirect. Drives UI hover tips that highlight the exact span.

schema.name: ChallengeRefsRendered
schema.Description: Pre-flattened Markdown bullet list of ChallengeRefs, so the dumb hbars template can print it verbatim (the engine has no loop-over-JSON helper).

schema.name: ChallengeNotes
schema.Description: Free-form Markdown comment: which challenge elements this relates to (directly or indirectly) and how it is load-bearing or illustrative.

schema.name: RefCount
schema.Description: Number of challenge refs (carried as raw for display; the hbars engine can't count a JSON string).

schema.name: AssignedLoop
schema.Description: FK -> LeopoldLoops.LeopoldLoopId; empty if unscheduled.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Display label.

schema.name: RelativePath
schema.Description: Path to this Features entry: /admin/features/<id>.

schema.name: MetaLine
schema.Description: One-line meta summary for the catalog (category - priority - ref count).

data.Title: Four-gate clinical-actionability keystone
data.Description: The entire system reduces to one computed boolean, `IndividualPredictions.IsClinicallyActionable`, TRUE only when all four derived gates hold (causally grounded, well-calibrated, non-spurious, ancestry-generalizable). Computed from a pure formula DAG, never entered. Seven synthetic patients land on both sides; each failure names the single deciding gate.

- **directly addresses** (prompt, `THE-ORIGINAL-CHALLENGE.md` L4:1608+68): _with calibrated uncertainty and no reliance on spurious correlations_

data.ChallengeNotes: This is the north-star reduction. It directly operationalizes the payoff paragraph's "individualized... ancestry-equitable... actionable prediction" and the prompt's demand for *calibrated uncertainty and no reliance on spurious correlations*. The four gates are the four hardest constraints in the prompt, each made a falsifiable boolean rather than a promise.
data.Name: Four-gate clinical-actionability keystone

data.Title: Trust boundary as a line in the DAG
data.Description: Any field with a formula belongs to the model; any raw-input field is the human's (or the LLM's, as an overridable front-end). Every Z-stat, gate, and the keystone is a pure inspectable formula; nothing above the leaves is enterable. The trust boundary is literally an edge in the dependency graph.

data.Description: LLM reads a natural-language case and writes raw intake observation rows (ancestry, allele freqs, presenting facts) to base tables. It interprets the case like an intake nurse and never computes a single inference above the leaf layer.

data.Title: Synthetic-but-transparent lab generator
data.Description: LLM emits the case's test results (effect sizes/SEs, replication signs/p-values, permutation effect sizes, calibration coverage) as visible, sourced, editable leaf rows. The numbers are fake (the case is invented) but every value is transparent, sourced to the case, and editable.

data.Title: Witnessed red->green inference harness (home screen)

Every category of inference (the DAG levels / the four gates) and the specific tests under each are asserted against the app's own API for all patients. As the model is built out, tests turn green; a fully green run is the proof.

data.Title: Treatment-response prediction via mechanism match
data.Description: A treatment-response prediction is trustworthy only when the treatment targets a confirmed causal mechanism (IsMechanismMatched) AND the treatment is effective. A drug aimed at a debunked mechanism, or one that didn't work / was adverse, is never 'predicted to respond'.

InferenceKinds.Description: Families of derivation the platform performs - the KINDS of inference in the DAG (lookups, aggregations, higher-order gates, state machines, transitive closure, predicate-gated narrative, cross-substrate conformance), independent of any one feature. Lets PLATFORM_FEATURES.md open with a short overview of the reasoning machinery before the per-feature catalog. Grounded in field types that actually exist in this rulebook; Maturity is Live unless an upstream step is still in flight.

schema.Description: A concrete field in the model that realizes this kind.

schema.name: EvidenceCount
schema.Description: How much of it exists (field counts / usage), for honest weight.

data.Title: Lookup / FK resolution
data.Description: Pull a related row's value across a foreign key so a derived field can reason over it in place (no application-side JOIN). The relationship is declared once; the value resolves wherever it is needed.
data.ExampleField: Individuals.FederatedDatasetNodeLabel (resolves the dataset's label by FK)
data.Name: Lookup / FK resolution

data.Title: Aggregation rollups
data.Description: Collapse many child rows into one parent value - counts, maxima, sums, fractions. This is how per-row facts become corpus-level patterns: a population signature is a rollup over each person's raw series, with no label assigned.
data.Name: Aggregation rollups

data.Title: Higher-order inference (gates over gates)
data.Description: Calculated fields that take other calculated fields as inputs, stacked into a multi-level DAG. The keystone is the apex: a boolean computed over four gates, each themselves computed over lower derivations - inference about inferences, never a hand-entered answer.
data.EvidenceCount: 200 calculated fields; the keystone is an AND of four derived gates
data.Name: Higher-order inference (gates over gates)

data.Title: State-machine derivation
data.Description: A subject's current state is derived from raw longitudinal inputs against a declared transition graph, not entered. Dwell time per state and the progression key fall out of the same derivation.
data.EvidenceCount: 2 state machines, 13 states, 14 transition rules; current state is computed
data.Name: State-machine derivation

data.Title: Transitive closure (reachability inference)
data.Description: Sparsely-asserted FromState->ToState edges imply the full reachability ordering, including never-asserted long-range pairs. The disease trajectory is inferred from the transition topology (e.g. intake -> actionable at hop 5, is_inferred=true) rather than hand-typed as an integer ladder - the autoimmune analogue of a step-1 -> step-5 ordering.
data.EvidenceCount: 1 closure field -> vw_state_transition_rules_closure: 14 asserted edges, 18 inferred pairs (to hop 5)
data.Name: Transitive closure (reachability inference)

data.Title: Predicate-gated narrative
data.Description: Each raw fact is turned into a phrase by a per-row formula that selects wording from its own value and a shared __meta__ phrasebook (e.g. a temperature becomes 'normal' | 'light fever' | 'severe fever'). The diagnosis writeup is assembled from these inspectable cells - no free-text generated at render time.
data.ExampleField: Individuals.ActivityTier / TemperatureNarrative (banded from the row's own value)
data.EvidenceCount: IF used 166x; per-row narrative fragment fields band raw values into language
data.Name: Predicate-gated narrative

data.Title: Cross-substrate conformance
data.Description: The same declared model is projected to a second substrate (OWL) and an independent reasoner closes it on its own; agreement with the Postgres recursive view is the conformance receipt. rulebook-to-owl emits the transition relation as an owl:TransitiveProperty, owlrl deduces every reachable pair, and owl/reason.py diffs that set against vw_state_transition_rules_closure - they match exactly (32 reachability pairs, incl. the inferred long-range intake -> actionable at hop 5). A recursive SQL view and an OWL-RL deductive closure compute the same reachability from one model: the reasoning is substrate-independent, not SQL-specific.
data.EvidenceCount: OWL-RL closure == Postgres closure: 14 asserted edges, 18 inferred pairs, 32 total - identical sets, verified by owl/reason.py
data.Name: Cross-substrate conformance

data.Definition: A literal, editable leaf value in the admin UI representing one choice the LLM (or a human) made; nudged, not re-prompted, when wrong.
data.Name: knob

data.Definition: The only two roles the LLM plays: interpret intake facts (clerk) and produce the case's test results (lab). Never the diagnostician.
data.Name: intake clerk + lab

data.Definition: The trust boundary, stated mechanically: fields with a formula belong to the model; raw-input leaf fields are the LLM's or a human's.
data.Name: line in the DAG

data.Definition: Input case text | extracted facts with per-fact provenance | deterministic diagnosis + gate trace — the two halves checkable independently.
data.Name: three-panel witness

data.Definition: Test results are synthetic (the case is invented) but every number is visible, sourced to the case, and editable; nothing is hidden.
data.Name: fake-but-transparent

data.Definition: The skeptic's claim that a deterministic function over LLM-chosen inputs just launders a hallucination; defeated by externalized knobs + independent witness.
data.Name: laundering objection

data.Definition: The v2 layer the audit asked for: disease modeled as a WITNESSED state machine whose transitions fire only on raw longitudinal observations (rising anti-dsDNA, falling C3/C4, proteinuria). Current state and dwell-time are derived & bitemporal — the contrast the audit drew with an 'evidence gate'.
data.Name: disease state simulator

data.Definition: The v1 layer: per-patient adjudication of whether a prediction is trustworthy enough to act on (the four gates + IsClinicallyActionable). v2 keeps this and adds the disease-state simulator above it on the same hub.
data.Name: evidence gate

data.Definition: A state machine on the polymorphic StateMachines substrate whose subject is a patient's disease course (presymptomatic -> serologic-active -> early-nephritis -> renal-flare-risk -> biopsy-indicated). Transitions are raw-leaf-triggered; current state is the derived IsCurrent occupancy.
data.Name: disease progression machine

data.Definition: Bitemporal derivation: how long a subject has been in a disease state (now - EnteredAt while current; ExitedAt - EnteredAt once left). The audit asked for 'how long has the patient/disease been in a state' — this is that, as a formula.
data.Name: dwell time

data.Definition: The raw longitudinal leaves driving progression: anti-dsDNA titre trend (rising/stable/falling), complement C3/C4 trend, proteinuria. The LLM/lab may emit these; everything above them is derived.
data.Name: serology trajectory

data.Definition: Derived field: given the patient's confirmed causal mechanism + current disease state, the recommended therapy (MMF vs belimumab vs anifrolumab) and the single deciding reason. The audit's second worked example.
data.Name: treatment line recommendation

data.Definition: The cohort-level surface where causal-hypothesis patterns EMERGE across many patients (vs v1's per-patient verdicts). Discovery is inherently corpus-level; this is the v2 reframe of the audit's 'you don't do discovery' critique.
data.Name: corpus discovery

data.Goal: Reframe: v1 was a per-patient evidence gate; v2 adds the disease-state layer. A disease progressing is a state machine (lupus-nephritis-progression: Presymptomatic then SerologicActive then EarlyNephritis then RenalFlareRisk then BiopsyIndicated) whose CURRENT state is DERIVED purely from raw serology leaves (rising anti-dsDNA + falling complement + proteinuria + sediment) - never hand-set. Bitemporal DwellDays answers the audit's 'how long in this state.' Same trust boundary: labs are the LLM's/clinician's, the state is the model's. Diego Santos's first worked example computes (derived SledaiScore 12, High/flare).

data.Title: Treatment-line recommendation + the disagreement counter-example
data.Goal: The audit's second worked example, computed: a derived RecommendedTreatmentLine + single TreatmentLineDecidingFactor (mycophenolate for active nephritis, anifrolumab for type-I-IFN, belimumab for autoantibody-driven, secukinumab for IL-17/23) - differentiated by confirmed mechanism x disease state, never by a label. The load-bearing proof: Diego is progression_vs_actionability_disagree = TRUE - the disease-state simulator says he IS progressing (BiopsyIndicated, high activity) while the actionability gate says NOT actionable (cryptic-relatedness leakage). A pure evidence gate could never produce that sentence; that it can is the proof v2 != v1 relabeled.

data.Title: Prune excess engineering - keep only what is load-bearing for the v1 audit response
data.Goal: A deliberate trimming pass (NOT a delete spree). Walk the model, the app, and the docs and find anything that was UPGRADED or SUPERSEDED and now lingers as a duplicate, plus any engineering that is not actually load-bearing toward the clearest pedagogical answer to the v1 audit. Candidates to scrutinise: superseded prediction types that don't earn their keep (e.g. a 4th parallel adverse-effect prediction once severity + treatment-response + treatment-line exist), redundant ontology slots that no longer carry a claim, and any view/endpoint with no consumer. Promotion rule inverted: if a thing is neither traceable to problem-to-solve.md nor load-bearing toward the keystone or the audit response, retire it. Goal: the clearest, most complete, DUPLICATE-FREE representation of the problem - so the audit response is concise as well as complete.
data.RuleCommitMsg: none - verification loop, no rule change; inventory found nothing load-bearing to retire
data.StateCommitMsg: state: Loop 12 - verified lean. Every endpoint is consumed (client or witnessed harness); no 4th adverse-effect prediction exists; the 3 zero-ref context tables (CounterfactualTrajectories/EnvironmentalExposures/TherapyOptions) are each named in problem-to-solve.md so they stay as honest schema coverage; no duplicate ontology slots or dead calc fields. The inverted promotion rule held: nothing is neither-traceable-nor-load-bearing.
data.StatusLine: - rule `none - verification loop, no rule change; inventory found nothing load-bearing to retire`; state `state: Loop 12 - verified lean. Every endpoint is consumed (client or witnessed harness); no 4th adverse-effect prediction exists; the 3 zero-ref context tables are each named in problem-to-solve.md so they stay as honest schema coverage; no duplicate ontology slots or dead calc fields.`
data.Name: Loop 12 — Prune excess engineering - keep only what is load-bearing for the v1 audit response

data.Title: LLM intake clerk + synthetic lab
data.Goal: FUTURE ROADMAP (not part of the v1-audit response - this is the framework-thesis deliverable, the next conversation). The trust-boundary payoff: wire the LLM to read a natural-language case and write ONLY leaf observations - intake facts + synthetic-but-transparent test results - with the three-panel witness (case text / extracted facts with per-fact provenance / derived diagnosis + gate trace). The LLM never computes a higher-order inference; every value it emits is an editable knob. Demotes the model to a replaceable, fully-overridable transcriber - the structural defeat of 'a hallucination laundered through a deterministic function.'
data.Name: Loop 13 — LLM intake clerk + synthetic lab

data.Title: Cohort-level equity report
data.Goal: FUTURE ROADMAP (traceable to the original challenge's equity requirement, but not part of the v1-audit response). Ancestry-equity made structural and corpus-level: calibration and actionability rates by ancestry (gates 2 and 4 of the keystone) as a derived dashboard, complementing the cohort discovery board. Equity is a checkable rate, not a promise.
data.Name: Loop 14 — Cohort-level equity report

schema.name: ReachableStateCount
schema.Description: Number of states reachable FROM this state via the transitive closure of the transition edges (rollup over vw_state_transition_rules_closure where from_id = this state). This is a graph-reachability fact the linear severity rank CANNOT reproduce: both Quiescent (the remission sink) and BiopsyIndicated (the terminal progression state) have 0 reachable-ahead, though they sit at opposite ends of the order — the count is non-monotonic in OrderIndex because the machine BRANCHES (remission). The closure is load-bearing here: delete it and this field is uncomputable.

schema.name: ProgressionClosure
schema.Description: Transitive closure of state-machine transitions (an owl:TransitiveProperty). The asserted FromState->ToState edges imply the full reachability ordering of each machine - including never-asserted long-range pairs such as PresymptomaticAutoimmunity -> BiopsyIndicated. Materialized by the transpiler as the cycle-safe recursive view vw_state_transition_rules_closure(from_id, to_id, hop_distance, is_inferred): asserted edges (hop 1) plus inferred reachability rows. The disease trajectory is derived from the transition topology, not hand-asserted.

__meta__.Description: General-purpose, global name/value settings ENTITY — deliberately transpiler-IGNORED (leading underscore). The one place for random/global/third-party/configuration values, choices, parameters, thresholds, and the reusable phrase atoms the derived report draws on. Just global key/value configuration data; that's it. Distinct from the `_meta` conversion-metadata section, and NOT a domain table (no FKs in, nothing aggregates over it, not in the DAG). Each row is one key/value setting. Narrative fragment fields on domain rows reference these keys so common language and shared numbers are authored and corrected once, never re-implemented per case. NOTE: banding-threshold rows (e.g. the temperature bands) are the numeric atoms; the per-case decision of which band applies lives in a calculated fragment field on the patient row, not here — this table holds the reusable pieces, not the case logic. See the effortless-meta-table skill.

data.Description: The diagnosis report is rendered by a thin Python renderer that concatenates already-derived, predicate-gated narrative fragment fields. Replaces the json-hbars-transform plan step. The renderer holds no narrative or business logic.
Report output format. markdown now -> pdf (diagnosis.pdf) once all harness tests are green.
Separator the renderer places between non-empty fragments when assembling a section (blank line = new paragraph).
When a fragment's predicate is false it renders empty; the renderer drops empty fragments so irrelevant language never appears for that case.
Literal global opener reused across every case's presenting-facts fragment. Authored once here, never re-typed per case.
Connector that introduces the derived conclusion sentence in the keystone fragment.
Reused phrase that names the one gate that decided the keystone, per ax-both-verdicts.
Reused fallback sentence for a section whose predicates all suppressed (used only where a deliberate, named absence is wanted rather than silence).

Lower bound (degF, inclusive) of the 'normal temperature' band. Referenced by the patient's TemperatureNarrative fragment to decide which band's language applies for that case. Illustrative of the banding pattern; replace with the clinically agreed value.
Upper bound (degF, inclusive) of the 'normal temperature' band; above this and up to fever-high is a light fever.
Upper bound (degF) of the 'light fever' band; strictly above this is a 'severe fever'.

Language emitted when the patient's temperature falls in the normal band. The TemperatureNarrative fragment picks one of the three band phrases by comparing the row's own value against the band-threshold rows.
Language emitted for the light-fever band (normal-high < temp <= fever-high).
Language emitted for the severe-fever band (temp > fever-high).
Total-coverage contract toggle: assert every meaningful patient token is traceable into a rendered fragment (tfs-report-covers-every-word).
Total-coverage contract toggle: assert every DAG node linked to the patient down to ground truth contributes a fragment or a named suppression (tfs-report-covers-every-dag-node).

_meta._CMCC_Summary: Loop 0: solve-by-inference rulebook for the Causal Autoimmune Architecture Platform. The keystone IndividualPredictions.IsClinicallyActionable is derived through a calculated-field DAG bottoming out only in raw observations. The per-patient diagnosis report is a derived, total-coverage projection: every patient word and every linked DAG node down to ground truth renders as predicate-gated structured natural language assembled from per-row narrative fragment fields (each one spreadsheet-cell simple), drawing reusable phrases and band thresholds from the __meta__ settings table.

data.Definition: Renal involvement of SLE; immune-complex glomerulonephritis. v2 models its PROGRESSION as a witnessed state machine driven by rising anti-dsDNA + falling complement + proteinuria.

data.Definition: Which therapy to choose given the patient's causal mechanism + disease state: mycophenolate (MMF) vs belimumab vs anifrolumab. v2 derives a recommended line + single deciding reason.

data.Definition: CNS/PNS manifestations of SLE (seizure, psychosis, cognitive). Modeled as a phenotype subtype carrying a SeverityScore and organ-domain tag.

data.Definition: Skin-limited or skin-predominant lupus (acute/subacute/chronic discoid). Phenotype subtype with organ-domain = skin.
data.Name: Cutaneous Lupus

data.Definition: Irreversible accumulated damage, SLICC-style. Modeled as a monotonic damage-accrual score distinct from reversible disease activity.
data.ChallengeStressorType: disease progression / feedback between progression and molecular state
data.Name: Organ Damage (SLICC/ACR Damage Index)

data.Definition: RF/anti-CCP-positive RA; stronger HLA shared-epitope + PTPN22 association, more erosive. Phenotype subtype with a serostatus tag.

data.Definition: Autoantibody-negative RA; distinct genetic architecture and trajectory. Phenotype subtype.

data.Definition: 'Sausage digit' — whole-digit swelling. PsA phenotype subtype.

data.Definition: Inflammatory eye disease overlapping spondyloarthritis. Phenotype subtype / extra-articular domain.

data.Definition: Gut inflammation overlapping spondyloarthritis (IL-23/IL-17 axis shared). Phenotype subtype representing cross-disease pleiotropy.

data.Definition: Responding to a flare. Workflow state tied to the flare-risk progression state.
data.Name: Workflow: Flare Management

data.Definition: Damage accrual, remission, outcomes over time. Workflow terminal state.
data.Name: Workflow: Long-Term Outcomes

SerologyObservations.Description: RAW longitudinal serology panels (the lab/LLM layer) + derived trend, SLEDAI sub-scores, and the disease state IMPLIED by each panel. The leaves that drive the lupus-nephritis progression machine.

schema.name: SerologyObservationId
schema.Description: Slug PK: sero-{ind}-{seq}.

schema.name: Individual
schema.Description: FK -> Individuals.IndividualId.

schema.name: ObservedAt
schema.Description: Valid-time of this serology panel (RAW leaf).

schema.name: SequenceIndex
schema.Description: 1-based order within the individual's panel series (RAW).

schema.name: AntiDsDnaIU
schema.Description: anti-dsDNA titre IU/mL (RAW lab).

schema.name: ComplementC3
schema.Description: C3 mg/dL (RAW lab).

schema.name: ComplementC4
schema.Description: C4 mg/dL (RAW lab).

schema.name: ProteinuriaGPerDay
schema.Description: urine protein g/day (RAW lab).

schema.name: EgfrMlMin
schema.Description: eGFR mL/min (RAW lab).

schema.name: HasActiveUrinarySediment
schema.Description: active casts/hematuria (RAW observation).

schema.name: PriorObservation
schema.Description: Self-FK -> the prior panel in this individual's series (seq-1). NULL for the first.

schema.name: PriorAntiDsDnaIU
schema.Description: Prior panel's dsDNA via PriorObservation FK, for trend.

schema.name: PriorC3
schema.Description: Prior C3 via PriorObservation FK.

schema.name: PriorC4
schema.Description: Prior C4 via PriorObservation FK.

schema.name: AntiDsDnaTrend
schema.Description: Rising/Falling/Stable vs prior panel (derived from raw).

schema.name: ComplementTrend
schema.Description: Rising/Falling/Stable on C3+C4 vs prior (derived).

schema.name: IsPreNephriticSignaturePanel
schema.Description: The pre-nephritic serology signature at THIS panel: rising anti-dsDNA + falling complement (the serological trajectory that precedes/tracks overt renal involvement). Emergent from the raw series, not a label anyone assigned. (Proteinuria is the OUTCOME the signature precedes, so it is deliberately NOT part of the signal.)

schema.name: IsSignificantProteinuria
schema.Description: proteinuria >= 0.5 g/day.

schema.name: IsNephroticRangeProteinuria
schema.Description: proteinuria >= 3.0 g/day.

schema.name: SledaiRenalPoints
schema.Description: SLEDAI-style renal sub-score (0/4/8) from proteinuria + sediment.

schema.name: SledaiSerologyPoints
schema.Description: SLEDAI-style serology sub-score (0/2/4) from low-complement + raised dsDNA.

schema.name: SledaiScore
schema.Description: Derived SLEDAI-style activity score = renal + serology points.

schema.name: ProgressionStateKey
schema.Description: Disease state IMPLIED by THIS panel (derived purely from raw leaves).

schema.name: ProgressionStateOrder
schema.Description: Numeric severity order of THIS panel's implied state (0..5). Lets the individual's current state be a MAXIFS over panels (highest state reached).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Echoes id.

schema.name: RelativePath
schema.Description: Path.

TherapyOptions.Description: Therapy lookup for the treatment-line-selection DAG (MMF/belimumab/anifrolumab/secukinumab).

schema.name: TherapyOptionId
schema.Description: Slug PK.

schema.name: TherapyLabel
schema.Description: Therapy name.

schema.name: TargetsPathway
schema.Description: Pathway this therapy targets (matches CausalMechanisms.TargetPathway).

schema.name: LineOrdinal
schema.Description: Treatment-line ordinal (1=first-line/induction).

schema.name: PreferredWhen
schema.Description: Clinical context where preferred.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Echo.

schema.name: RelativePath
schema.Description: Path.
